touches.ai_summary: Inbound request from Ruby Tanaka (Head of RevOps) expressing interest in onboarding automation. Ruby mentioned current manual processes are slowing down their sales team's ramp time.
next_steps: Schedule qualification call to understand current state and requirements

touches.ai_summary: Qualification call with Ruby. Discussed QuantumSolutions' current onboarding challenges—32 reps manually creating materials, inconsistent messaging. Ruby confirmed 278 employees, mid-market segment, looking at automation tier. Strong engagement, clear pain points around rep productivity.

questions_asked.question: What's your current onboarding process for new sales reps?
questions_asked.answer: Mostly manual—managers create decks and documents, takes 2-3 weeks before reps are productive

questions_asked.question: What kind of timeline are you working with for a solution?
questions_asked.answer: Ideally something in place by end of Q2, our hiring ramp starts Q3

next_steps: Ruby to gather current onboarding materials
Schedule follow-up to discuss automation scope

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up qualification call. Ruby shared examples of current onboarding decks and training docs. Discussed scope: 32 seats for sales team, unlimited automation tier to handle multiple playbooks. Ruby mentioned Nathan Schulz (Sales Manager) would be key end user.

questions_asked.question: Who else should be involved in evaluating this?
questions_asked.answer: Nathan runs the sales team day-to-day, and Ivy Almeida our SVP of Revenue will need to sign off

questions_asked.question: Can your platform handle different playbooks for different product lines?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, unlimited automation tier supports multiple workflows and custom templates

next_steps: Deep dive discovery call to map out automation workflows
Ruby to brief Nathan on the solution

touches.ai_summary: Discovery call with Ruby. Detailed walkthrough of QuantumSolutions' three product lines and corresponding onboarding needs. Ruby emphasized need for flexibility—each product has different sales motion. Discussed integration requirements with their Salesforce instance and LMS. Strong business case emerging around time-to-productivity.

questions_asked.question: What does success look like for this project?
questions_asked.answer: Cut onboarding time from 3 weeks to under 1 week, consistent messaging across all reps

questions_asked.question: How does the solution integrate with Salesforce and our existing LMS?
questions_asked.answer: Native Salesforce integration, LMS connection via API or embed

questions_asked.question: What's your budget range for this initiative?
questions_asked.answer: We're flexible for the right solution, probably in the $25-35K range annually

objections: Need to make sure this doesn't add complexity for managers who already have limited time
next_steps: Ruby to audit all current onboarding assets
Schedule technical discovery for integrations
Prepare demo focused on multi-playbook automation

touches.ai_summary: Quick qualification check-in. Ruby confirmed she's compiled the onboarding asset inventory—over 40 different documents and decks currently in use. Reinforced urgency due to Q3 hiring plans (12 new reps). Confirmed Nathan and Ivy are aligned on exploring this solution.

questions_asked.question: Have you looked at other solutions for this?
questions_asked.answer: Not actively—this came through our network and the timing is right
